name: New Alphabet
description: New Alphabet is a Crouwelian design constitution for the web: a strict, typographic, grid-governed alternative to vibe-coded products.

userStories.title: Write constitution manifesto
userStories.description: Write the constitution manifesto that states the anti-vibe-coded thesis, core philosophy, aesthetic position, goals, and anti-pattern posture in one coherent language.
acceptanceCriteria: The manifesto states the anti-vibe-coded thesis, core philosophy, goals, non-goals, and aesthetic position.
Naming, layer model, and anti-pattern posture are explicit enough to guide implementation.
The language is exact, restrained, and systemic rather than promotional.
Code remains terse, constrained, and purposeful.
Implementation follows New Alphabet's doctrine rather than generic framework habits.
Completed work is committed locally and pushed to remote with a small technical commit carrying a random Crouwel quote or theme.

userStories.title: Implement token and grid system
userStories.description: Implement the token and grid system so the constitutional rules for spacing, type, density, and alignment become concrete runtime law.
acceptanceCriteria: The token and grid system defines spacing, type, density, and alignment rules clearly enough for runtime use.
Arbitrary layout values are structurally disallowed by the design system shape.
The foundation rules are documented and ready to support primitives and recipes.
Code remains terse, constrained, and purposeful.
Implementation follows New Alphabet's doctrine rather than generic framework habits.

The component layer proves that serious product UI can be built without broad optionality.

project.name: New Alphabet
project.tagline: A Crouwelian design-system engine for Rust, Leptos, and agentic product building.

executive_summary.problem: Most UI libraries optimize for breadth, styling flexibility, and human improvisation. They do not provide a strict visual grammar, a narrow system of composition, or an installable agent-facing design constitution that an LLM can reliably build from.
executive_summary.solution: Build New Alphabet as a Rust-first, Leptos-native framework for web blogs and web-based SaaS, with a strict foundational grammar, constrained primitives, semantic components, recipe-level patterns, and an agent-readable contract surface exposed through code, CLI, and protocol-friendly tooling.
why_now: Rust and Leptos now make a serious full-stack Rust web framework viable.
AI coding sessions are becoming normal, but they lack strong installable design constitutions.
Teams need repeatable systems for content-heavy and workflow-heavy web products.
A sharply opinionated OSS offering is more memorable than another generic component library.
executive_summary.release_thesis: New Alphabet should launch as both a real framework and a live system an LLM can load into a coding session to plan, scaffold, implement, validate, and explain product UIs with low drift.

core_thesis.statement: New Alphabet is a Crouwelian design constitution for the web: a strict, typographic, grid-governed alternative to vibe-coded products.
problem_reading: The failure in modern product UI is not only taste but the absence of a governing language.
Vibe-coded products drift because each screen is composed from local decisions instead of system law.
AI-assisted implementation amplifies this drift when the system cannot be loaded as an explicit design contract.
answer: Establish one explicit doctrine for layout, hierarchy, components, states, and recipes.
Implement that doctrine in Rust and Leptos rather than leaving it as doc-only guidance.
Expose the doctrine through CLI and agent surfaces so scaffolding, explanation, and validation operate on the real system.

rejects: Prompt-to-prompt layout invention with no stable grammar.
Component-library defaults masquerading as product identity.
Local taste decisions that accumulate into drift.
Open-ended styling freedom that weakens family resemblance.
favors: A recognizable family resemblance across many surfaces.

doctrine_hierarchy: The agent contract exposes the doctrine.

litmus_tests: If a surface could be mistaken for a standard component-library demo, the system is being applied too loosely.
If a screen requires decorative exceptions to feel finished, the underlying structure is not carrying enough weight.
If editorial and SaaS surfaces do not feel like members of one family, the grammar is not strong enough.

core_philosophy.order_before_ornament: Structure precedes style. The interface must first clarify what this surface is, what matters, how parts relate, and what action follows.
core_philosophy.grid_as_operating_law: The grid governs layout, spacing, hierarchy, and rhythm. It is constitutional, not decorative.
core_philosophy.information_over_personality: The UI exists to make relationships legible. Character may emerge, but communication remains primary.
core_philosophy.constraint_generates_freedom: A reduced design vocabulary is a strength. Precision and composition replace arbitrary optionality.
core_philosophy.systems_over_pages: The core artifact is not a pretty screen but a repeatable language that can generate many coherent screens.
core_philosophy.medium_truth: Loading, empty, error, focus, responsiveness, semantics, accessibility, and maintainability are part of design, not implementation afterthoughts.
core_philosophy.agents_need_rules_not_vibes: New Alphabet must be consumable by humans and LLMs through explicit schemas, composition rules, state contracts, and validation gates.

primary: Build a Rust-first framework for coherent web blogs and web-based SaaS.
Use Leptos as the core application framework for SSR, hydration, and shared Rust logic.
Encode a strict Crouwelian design grammar across foundation, primitives, semantic components, and recipes.
Provide a CLI that can scaffold, inspect, validate, and explain New Alphabet structures.
Provide an agent-facing contract surface that an LLM can load into a coding session and build from.
Ship a serious first public OSS release with a strong README, docs, examples, release discipline, and contribution posture.
secondary: Demonstrate one grammar powering both editorial/blog and SaaS/workflow surfaces.
Support sparse-prompt and highly-directed user workflows equally well.
Make the framework feel like a school of design rather than a generic toolkit.
Prepare the system for future agent validation and recipe synthesis.

non_goals: Do not compete with broad component ecosystems on total component count.
Do not support unlimited styling freedom at V0.
Do not reproduce only Crouwel's look without his systemic rigor.
Do not treat AI as a source of decorative randomization.
Do not build native mobile or desktop runtimes in the initial release.
Do not attempt multi-framework adapters before the Leptos implementation is stable.
Do not promise a full autonomous design agent at V0.

primary_personas.name: Solo Rust Builder
needs: Strong visual system without designing from scratch
Fast path from idea to coherent blog or SaaS

primary_personas.name: Product Engineer
needs: Predictable APIs and low design drift

primary_personas.name: AI-Assisted Builder
needs: Installable framework context in an LLM coding session

agentic: Attach New Alphabet to a Codex session and scaffold a product from user intent
Validate whether generated code conforms to New Alphabet rules
Explain which recipe or primitive should be used for a requested page
Transform a natural-language product description into framework-native implementation tasks

distribution_surfaces.purpose: Actual Rust and Leptos implementation of foundations, primitives, semantic components, and recipes.
Human and agent entry point for init, add, plan, validate, inspect, explain, and scaffold commands.
Machine-readable schemas, recipes, constraints, examples, and validation logic that can be loaded into an AI coding session.

human: Create a new Leptos app using New Alphabet templates or add New Alphabet to an existing project.
Use CLI commands to scaffold common layouts and recipes.
Use docs and examples to understand the system.
llm_session: Attach New Alphabet to the coding session via local repo, packaged context bundle, or future protocol adapter.
Allow the model to inspect foundations, primitives, semantic components, recipes, rules, and anti-patterns.
Permit the model to scaffold and implement inside defined boundaries rather than inventing structure from scratch.

stack.styling_strategy: framework-controlled tokens and class generation with strict composition rules
stack.schema_format: JSON for canonical contracts; optional Rust types generated or mirrored from schema
stack.documentation_site: New Alphabet site built with New Alphabet itself where possible

layers.name: foundation
layers.description: Grid, spacing, type, density, color roles, borders, motion rules, breakpoints, semantics.

layers.name: structural_primitives
layers.description: Low-level layout building blocks that define the constitutional geometry of a screen.

layers.name: semantic_components
layers.description: Meaning-bearing UI elements for actual product work.

layers.name: recipes
layers.description: Opinionated, reusable full-screen and multi-region compositions for blogs and SaaS.

layers.name: agent_contract_surface
layers.description: Machine-readable formalization of the first four layers for agent use.

governing_principles: The grid decides major layout before individual components are chosen.
Tokens are constitutional inputs rather than optional theme values.
Semantics outrank appearance when naming or composing UI.
Recipes are approved compositions, not marketing templates.

grid.role: Constitutional geometry for page and region layout.
must_forbid: Ad hoc layout rules outside named grid behavior

spacing_scale.role: Single spacing law for rhythm and separation.
must_forbid: One-off spacing fixes for individual screens

type_scale.role: Shared typographic hierarchy across editorial and SaaS surfaces.
must_define: Display, heading, body, and annotation roles

density_modes.role: Named intensity modes for information density.
must_define: At least calm, regular, and dense modes or their equivalents
How density changes spacing, type, and table behavior

color_roles.role: Semantic color usage rather than expressive palette freedom.
must_define: Canvas, text, accent, border, status, and emphasis roles
must_forbid: Color as the only carrier of meaning
Unlimited variant color theming at V0

motion_rules.role: Purposeful motion that clarifies change rather than decorates it.

breakpoints.role: Responsive adaptation of the same grammar.
must_define: How rails and detail panes adapt

state_tokens.role: Shared visual language for loading, error, success, focus, and disabled states.

must_not_be: A decorative wrapper with no structural responsibility

PageGrid.role: Named page-level grid for region placement.

Region.role: Primary semantic area of a screen.

Rail.role: Persistent side structure for navigation, filtering, or utility content.
must_not_be: A place for arbitrary overflow content

must_support: Reusability across editorial and SaaS surfaces

Panel.role: Bounded structural surface for grouped content or action.

notes: Variants should be semantic rather than purely visual.
Primary action weight should come from hierarchy, not palette sprawl.
Label, help, and error relationships must be explicit.
Use when choices are finite and named.
Do not use as a visual styling workaround.
Color cannot be the only signal.
Labels must remain meaningful without surrounding context.

InlineAlert.role: Contextual message inside workflow or reading flow.
notes: Severity should be obvious through structure and label, not only color.

EmptyState.role: Structured response to absence of content or results.
notes: Must explain absence and possible next action.
Must not collapse into generic placeholder copy.
Truncation, wrapping, and alignment rules must be explicit.
Table density must map to named density modes.
Actions should remain finite and hierarchically ordered.
Do not overload with secondary controls.
Must define relation to source list or table.
Should preserve hierarchy when data is absent.

adaptation_limits: Must remain readable as an archive rather than a marketing landing page.
The reading flow must stay primary.

DocsShell.intent: Documentation surface with navigation and reading flow.

SearchResultsWorkspace.intent: Operational surface for search, filtering, and results review.

ReviewQueue.intent: Dense review workflow with queue, detail, and action logic.
adaptation_limits: Must remain legible under dense operational use.

SettingsWorkspace.intent: Configuration surface with section navigation and editable panels.

foundations: Use plain names for rules and scales rather than branded terms.
Name tokens by role, not by appearance alone.
primitives: Use structural nouns such as Rail, Region, Panel, or Stack.
Do not name primitives after visual style.
semantic_components: Use role-based names such as Table or StatusBadge.
Do not name components after decoration or palette.
recipes: Use product-surface names such as ArticleShell or ReviewQueue.
A recipe name should imply intent, not ornament.

top_level_layout.purpose: Core types, tokens, schema models, shared contracts, and low-level utilities.
Foundational design rules: grid, spacing, type, density, color roles, motion, borders.
Structural layout primitives implemented for Leptos.
Semantic components built on top of primitives.
Canonical JSON schemas, validation models, and Rust type mirrors.
CLI for init, add, plan, inspect, explain, and validate.
Agent-facing helpers and future protocol integration layer.
Public documentation site and design manifesto.
Small, focused examples for primitives, components, recipes, and integration paths.
Published machine-readable contracts and example context bundles for agent sessions.
Prompt templates, spec examples, and agent session bootstrap materials.
Release, schema export, validation, and contributor automation.

initial_commands.purpose: Create a new project or add New Alphabet to an existing Leptos project.
Scaffold a recipe such as blog-index, article-shell, settings-workspace, or review-queue.
Add a semantic component with required states and foundation bindings.
Turn a natural-language product request into a New Alphabet-native plan.
Explain a primitive, component, recipe, or rule in framework terms.
Inspect project usage and identify which primitives, components, and recipes are present.
Validate conformance to schema, composition, state, spacing, and accessibility rules.
Generate an agent-readable bundle containing schema, recipes, examples, and constraints for a coding session.

future_agent_tools.purpose: Return current grid, spacing, type, density, and color-role rules.
Return all primitives with slots, constraints, and examples.
Return all semantic components with states, variants, and composition rules.
Return recipe-level patterns matched to user intents.
Convert a natural-language request into a framework-native implementation plan.
Check whether a proposed composition violates framework rules.
Generate New Alphabet-compliant starter code from recipe or intent.

design_principles: File writes should be explicit and visible.
Read-only commands should not mutate project state.
The CLI should operationalize the doctrine rather than bypass it.

command_contracts.side_effect_profile: writes project setup and framework wiring
must_do: Create or update only the files needed to initialize New Alphabet.
Explain what was created or changed.
Preserve existing project code where possible.
Generate only approved recipe structures.
Bind recipe output to foundations and primitives.
Include required states where relevant.
Generate semantic components rather than visual wrappers.
Include explicit state coverage and accessibility hooks.
Keep prop surfaces finite.
Return a structured implementation plan.
Choose recipes or primitives before proposing custom layout.
Explain why the plan fits the doctrine.
Explain primitives, components, recipes, or rules in framework terms.
Cite constraints, allowed compositions, and required states.
Map project code back to New Alphabet structures.
Identify drift from approved primitives, components, and recipes.
Report schema, composition, spacing, state, and accessibility violations.
Suggest repairs in framework terms.
Differentiate errors from warnings.
Generate a versioned machine-readable context bundle.
Export the real doctrine, not a simplified alias.
Remain usable without network dependency.

output_principles: Prefer short, exact output over verbose narration.
Surface next steps only when they are materially useful.
write_policy: Commands that write files must say so before or while doing it.
Scaffolds should prefer additive or local edits over sweeping rewrites.
Generated files should be valid starting points rather than placeholders.

layout: All serious pages must be composed from primitives before semantic embellishment.
Arbitrary spacing values are forbidden outside the token scale.
Components must align to the grid and declared density mode.
Recipes may constrain allowed region combinations.
api_discipline: Components must expose minimal prop surfaces.
Variants must be explicit and finite.
Invalid combinations should be impossible or loudly rejected.
Slot names must be stable and semantically clear.
typography: Hierarchy should be carried primarily by type, spacing, and alignment.
Color and ornament are secondary signals.
Editorial and SaaS surfaces share one underlying typographic grammar.
statefulness: Every serious component must define default, disabled, loading, empty, error, and success states where applicable.
Interactive components must define focus-visible behavior and keyboard rules.
Overflow and truncation behavior must be explicit for dense surfaces.
accessibility: Accessible names and descriptions are required.
Color is never the sole carrier of meaning.
Focus order and focus visibility are system-level concerns.

visual: Component-library default aesthetics used as product identity.
Decorative exceptions added to rescue weak structure.
Color or ornament doing work that hierarchy should do.
layout: Major page regions invented outside primitives or recipes.
Arbitrary spacing values used to solve local problems.
Rails, panels, or detail panes used as generic overflow containers.
api: Variants defined by visual mood instead of semantic role.
implementation: Optional escape hatches that weaken the system at call sites.
agentic: Prompt-driven layout invention with no recipe or primitive justification.
Context exports that omit the real constraints.
Validation that reports style opinions instead of constitutional rules.

data_dense_rules: Tables and lists must define truncation and wrapping rules.
Detail panes must define loading and unavailable states.
Filter structures must define zero-result states.

agent_model.design_principle: A model should be able to operate from sparse or highly specific user prompts because New Alphabet supplies the formal language underneath.

supported_guidance_levels.example: Build me a content-heavy SaaS for reviewing submissions.
Build a dense review workspace with left navigation rail, center results table, right detail pane, persistent action strip, loading and dirty states, and a calm editorial tone.

ideal_outcomes: The LLM can choose an appropriate recipe from a user description.
The LLM can scaffold valid Leptos code from framework rules.
The LLM can explain why a chosen composition is valid.
The LLM can validate or repair drift from New Alphabet's constitution.

generation_sequence: Choose a recipe or primitive composition before writing UI code.
Select semantic components that satisfy the required states.
Generate only valid structures and finite variants.
Run validation and explain the result in framework terms.
repair_sequence: Identify which constitutional rule is being violated.
Prefer narrowing or deleting invalid structure before adding new abstractions.
hard_rules: Do not invent layout outside the defined primitive and recipe model unless the PRD is explicitly expanded.
Do not weaken the doctrine to make generation easier.
Do not hide uncertainty behind decorative output.

agent_roles.name: Constitution Architect
responsibilities: Define and refine philosophy, naming, grid, spacing, type, density, color roles, and anti-patterns.

agent_roles.name: Foundation Implementer
responsibilities: Implement tokens and low-level rules in Rust.
Ensure foundations are SSR-safe and framework-wide.

agent_roles.name: Primitive Builder
responsibilities: Build and test structural primitives in Leptos.
Constrain layout composition and slot behavior.

agent_roles.name: Semantic Component Builder
responsibilities: Build semantic components with explicit states, variants, and accessibility support.
Keep prop surfaces minimal and deterministic.

agent_roles.name: Recipe Composer
responsibilities: Compose full-screen recipes for blog and SaaS use cases.
Document recipe intent and allowed adaptations.

agent_roles.name: Contract and CLI Builder
responsibilities: Implement schema export, validation, CLI commands, and agent context bundles.

agent_roles.name: Quality Critic
responsibilities: Run design, API, accessibility, documentation, and state-completeness gates.

agent_roles.name: OSS Steward
responsibilities: Prepare README, roadmap, contributor docs, issue templates, releases, and examples.

workstreams.name: Constitution and Naming
workstreams.objective: Define the visual and conceptual laws of New Alphabet.

workstreams.name: Foundations and Primitives
workstreams.objective: Implement the base grammar in Rust and Leptos.

workstreams.name: Semantic Component Layer
workstreams.objective: Create the smallest useful set of semantic product components.

workstreams.name: Recipes and Demonstrators
workstreams.objective: Prove that one grammar can serve both editorial and SaaS surfaces.

workstreams.name: CLI and Agent Contract Surface
workstreams.objective: Make New Alphabet installable and queryable in human and agent workflows.

milestones.name: Constitution Freeze
outcomes: Grid, spacing, type, density, and color-role rules documented

milestones.name: Foundation Runtime
outcomes: Foundation crates compile and are documented
Primitive layout system exists and renders in Leptos

milestones.name: Dual Demonstrator Proof
outcomes: Demo blog built from New Alphabet
Demo SaaS workspace built from New Alphabet

milestones.name: CLI and Context Export
outcomes: CLI init, add, explain, validate, and export commands function
Agent-readable context bundle can be generated

design_gate: Every primitive aligns to the declared grid and spacing rules.
Typography and hierarchy remain coherent across editorial and SaaS examples.
Recipes visibly read as one family.
aesthetic_gate: Surfaces should be recognizably New Alphabet rather than generic contemporary SaaS compositions.
Crouwelian logic should remain visible even when the surface is modernized.
Editorial and SaaS examples should feel like members of one family without visual mimicry.
api_gate: Component APIs are narrow and documented.
Unsupported prop combinations are blocked or rejected.
state_gate: Required states are implemented and documented.
No production component ships with undefined empty, error, or loading behavior where relevant.
accessibility_gate: Semantics and accessible naming are validated.
platform_gate: Responsive behavior is defined for required recipes.
agent_gate: Schemas exist for foundations, primitives, core components, and recipes.
CLI can export a context bundle.
At least three prompt-to-plan examples are demonstrated.
docs_gate: README explains philosophy and quick start clearly.
Docs site covers foundations, primitives, components, recipes, CLI, and agent workflows.
Examples are runnable and mapped to docs.

should_ship: Plan command from natural language intent
later: Autonomous multi-agent orchestration built into the project

v0.theme: Prove the doctrine through runtime, demos, CLI, and contract export.

v0_5.theme: Tighten validation and deepen recipes without broadening the system excessively.

v1.theme: Broaden the system only after the doctrine is stable and legible.

v2.theme: Deeper agent planning and carefully bounded extension mechanisms.
includes: Limited theme families that preserve the constitution

design_and_doctrine: The demo blog and demo SaaS read as one family without looking identical.
No V0 example depends on arbitrary spacing or ad hoc styling escape hatches.
Editorial and operational surfaces both feel recognizably New Alphabet.
runtime_and_tooling: Schemas cover foundations, primitives, core components, and V0 recipes.
Context export contains enough information for prompt-to-plan and prompt-to-scaffold workflows.
CLI scaffolding and validation work without network dependency.
docs_and_release: README, docs site, examples, prompts, and exported context agree on naming and layer model.
Contribution guidance, release notes template, issue templates, and license are present at public launch.

performance: Reasonable SSR performance for demo surfaces.
Hydration cost kept modest for editorial surfaces.
doctrinal_quality: No V0 surface should read as generic component-library output.
The framework should remain recognizable across editorial and SaaS surfaces.
Adaptability should happen through approved structures rather than ad hoc styling.
reliability: Strict typing for contracts and component APIs.
Schema validation integrated into development workflow.
Deterministic examples and scaffold outputs where feasible.
security: CLI must not execute arbitrary untrusted instructions by default.
Scaffolding and export commands must be explicit about file writes.
No network-dependent behavior required for basic framework use.
maintainability: Schema and implementation parity must be maintained.
Recipes should not bypass foundations and primitives.

voice_guardrails: Write like a constitution and a reference rather than a marketing page.
Avoid inflated language that makes the system sound broader than it is.
required_supporting_artifacts: Contributor guidance that protects the doctrine

risks.mitigation: Keep V0 narrow and implementation-led. Ship blog and SaaS demos early.
Treat component count as secondary to grammar quality.
Continuously test whether the system reflects Crouwel's logic rather than merely his visual aura.
Provide scaffolds, examples, and clear docs. Make the strict path legible.
Ship schemas and CLI export early; defer protocol complexity until the contract surface is real.

decision_log.reason: The framework, CLI, schemas, demos, docs, and agent surface must evolve together.

decision_log.decision: Use Rust and Leptos for the initial implementation.
decision_log.reason: This preserves one-language coherence while targeting the web seriously.

decision_log.decision: Support both blog and SaaS at V0.
decision_log.reason: The framework's thesis is that one grammar should power both content and software.

decision_log.decision: Include CLI and exported context bundles at V0.
decision_log.reason: Installability into human and agent workflows is central to the product idea, not a later garnish.

decision_log.decision: Treat the doctrine as primary and the CLI and agent surface as downstream.
decision_log.reason: New Alphabet should not dilute into an operational system with no aesthetic center.

decision_log.decision: Favor sharpness over breadth at V0.
decision_log.reason: The system will only be persuasive if its grammar is exact before it becomes broad.

project_done_when: A developer can create or add New Alphabet to a Leptos project through the CLI.
A developer or LLM can scaffold at least one blog surface and one SaaS surface from the framework.
Foundations, primitives, core components, and recipes are documented and schema-backed.
Validation can detect meaningful drift from the framework's rules.
The repository reads as a coherent public offering with docs, examples, roadmap, and release structure.
v0_done_when: M0 through M5 milestones are complete.
Demo blog and demo SaaS clearly prove the thesis.
Agent context export works and is documented.

seed_backlog.title: Implement AppShell, PageGrid, Region, Rail, Stack, Row, ColumnGroup, Panel
Implement Button, TextField, Select, StatusBadge, InlineAlert, EmptyState, Table
Implement schema bundle and validation engine
Build docs site using New Alphabet

example_user_prompts: Build me a blog for longform essays and notes using New Alphabet.
Build me a B2B admin workspace for reviewing submissions with a left rail, dense table, and right-side detail pane.
Use New Alphabet to scaffold a settings workspace with section navigation and editable panels.
Explain which recipe I should use for a documentation site with article pages and archive navigation.
Validate whether this page structure conforms to New Alphabet's grid and state rules.

open_questions.question: How strict should the initial token scales be before real demos expose edge cases?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The doctrine needs enough precision to govern implementation without freezing poor early choices.

open_questions.question: What is the exact schema format for recipe composition and validation errors?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The agent contract depends on stable, machine-readable rules rather than prose interpretation.

open_questions.question: How much responsive adaptation should recipes handle before a new recipe is required?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The system needs bounded adaptability rather than hidden layout freedom.

open_questions.question: Which parts of the CLI should produce machine-readable JSON output at V0 versus later?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Agent workflows benefit from structured output, but premature protocol design could slow the core system.

open_questions.question: How much theming, if any, can be introduced later without dissolving the doctrine?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Future expansion should not turn the framework into a generic design-token engine.

open_questions.question: What is the narrowest acceptable path from prompt to scaffold that still proves the agent thesis?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The project should prove installability without promising autonomous design intelligence too early.
